videos.title: Peachy vibes here 🍑 at the Veolia Atlanta Pickleball Championships 🏆 presented by Ensure
Let’s paint some lines in ATL 🍑🙌 Round of 64 starts TOMORROW!!
Have you seen the trailer for PARTNERS yet? 📺 Series premiers on May 5th on YouTube! 🙌
New to Major League Pickleball? Watch this first.
videos.description:
It’s not just pickleball, it’s team pickleball.
Coed lineups. City pride. Matches that come down to a final DreamBreaker™.

This is Major League Pickleball, where team chemistry is the difference maker and every event builds toward the hardest trophy to win in the sport. 

20 teams. The 2026 season takes place over 13 events this summer. Join us at an event or tune-in and catch your favorite team.

videos.title: 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers
videos.description:
Tune in starting at 10am ET as the 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers unfolds live. Dave Fleming and Will Daughton are breaking down the first 28 picks as the first two male and two female roster slots are filled for each team. Expect real-time reactions, insights, and a front row seat to understanding teams' selections.
⁠
The remaining 38 selections and complete rosters will be posted in real-t

videos.title: Inside MLP - 2026 Draft Rules and Preview
videos.description:
In this edition of Inside MLP, Will Daughton explains the new rules and parts of the 2026 MLP Draft, previews every team's pre-draft roster, and showcases the MLP Draft Show. All that and more on Inside MLP!

Stay connected and never miss an update!
👉 Live scoring, player stats, and event info: https://www.majorleaguepickleball.co
👇 Join our community on social!
Instagram: instagram.com/MajorLea

videos.title: Inside MLP - 2026 Player Keeper Reactions
videos.description:
In this episode of Inside MLP, Dave Fleming and Will Daughton provide their initial reactions to the Player Keeper Deadline and discuss how some decisions could have massive implications for the upcoming Major League Pickleball draft on Feb. 27.

See the full list of Keepers by team here: https://majorleaguepickleball.co/news/major-league-pickleball-announces-2026-player-keepers/. 


Stay connecte

videos.title: 2026 USA Pickleball Golden Ticket Recap | San Diego, CA
videos.description:
🏆 2026 USA Pickleball Golden Ticket Tournament – San Diego, CA. | Pickleball Tournament Recap

Relive the action from the 2026 USA Pickleball Golden Ticket Tournament in San Diego, CA, presented by TRZ Recovery held April 22-26 at the iconic Barnes Tennis Center! This highlight reel showcases the intense rallies, standout sportsmanship and the vibrant USA Pickleball community atmosphere that make

videos.title: Forehand vs. Backhand in Pickleball
videos.description:
Every shot in pickleball, whether it’s a dink, drop, drive, volley, or overhead, is either a forehand or a backhand. In this video, we break down the fundamentals of forehands vs. backhands so you can better understand the differences and apply them to every shot in your game.

Forehands are hit on your dominant side. If you’re a right-handed player, that’s your right side; if you’re left-handed,

videos.title: How to Hit a Third Shot Drop
videos.description:
The third shot drop is one of the most important shots in pickleball and a key to turning defense into offense. In this video, we break down how the serving team can use the third shot drop to neutralize their disadvantage and safely move up to the kitchen.

Because of the double bounce rule, the serving team must stay back after the serve, while the returning team can immediately advance to the n

videos.title: Elevating the Golden Ticket Experience with Badges and Medals
videos.description:
USA Pickleball is raising the bar for the athlete experience, right down to the moment players step onto the medal stand.

Through a new partnership with Badges and Medals, USA Pickleball has introduced custom, one-of-a-kind medals across its Golden Ticket tournaments, giving athletes a meaningful, tangible way to celebrate their performance and remember their journey on the Path to Nationals.

items.title: PPA Tour 2026 Atlanta Pickleball Championships: Draws, schedule and storylines
items.summary: The Atlanta Pickleball Championships is annually one of the biggest events on the PPA Tour calendar, and the action started Monday with pro qualifying. More

items.summary: The United Pickleball Association of America recently released its first official rulebook that specifically applies to the PPA Tour and Major League Pickleball. More

items.summary: This is a preview of the 2026 PPA Tour Veolia Atlanta Pickleball Championships, being held April 27 - May 3, at the Life Time Peachtree Corners.
items.author: Erik Tice

items.title: 4 Tote Bags Perfectly Suited for a Day on the Pickleball Court
items.summary: Roomy. Stylish. Practical. Everyone's sleeping on the pickleball tote bag. But trust on this: Try one of these and you may never go back.
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: The Pickleball Counterattack: Paula Rives' Pro Technique Explained
items.summary: PPA pro Paula Rives breaks down the pickleball counterattack, the shot that turns defense into offense. Learn the footwork, positioning, and anticipation needed to neutralize aggressive bangers.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Perfect 60-Minute Pickleball Practice Plan
items.summary: Professional pickleball player John Cincola shares the exact pickleball practice plan he uses to train touring pros and beginners alike.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Build Stamina for Pickleball: 4-Week Cardio and Practice Plan
items.summary: Building stamina for pickleball requires more than just logging extra court hours, it takes a structured cardio and movement plan. This 4-week guide gives you the exact training framework to outlast your opponents and play stronger in every game.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Exactly When to Hit a Third Shot Drop in Pickleball
items.summary: The third shot drop is one of pickleball's most critical shots, but knowing when to deploy it separates casual players from competitive ones. Understanding the exact moments to hit a third shot drop versus a drive can transform your court positioning and win rate.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Stay Solid Under Pickleball Pressure
items.summary: The gap between pros and amateurs isn't skill—it's how they handle pickleball pressure. PPA pro Ashley Griffith breaks down five critical habits that separate champions from the rest.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Pickleball Butt: What It Is, Why It Happens, and How to Get Back on Court Safely
items.summary: “Pickleball butt” usually refers to deep buttock or hip pain from piriformis irritation, sciatic nerve irritation, high hamstring issues, or hip overload. Rest, modify play, strengthen hips/glutes gradually, and get checked if pain shoots down the leg, causes weakness, limping, o
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Two-Handed Backhand Speed-Up That Catches Everyone Off Guard
items.summary: The two-handed backhand down-the-line speed-up is a doubles attack used when an opponent cheats toward the middle. Stay low, contact the ball in front, use a compact swing, aim inside the sideline, and hit with controlled pace and topspin rather than full power. A two-handed back
items.author: Ana

items.title: Pro Pickleball Has a New Rulebook, Here’s What Changed
items.summary: The UPA-A released a 71-page document detailing everything from a new card system governing on-court behavior to paddle challenges, line calls, and yes – blowing the ball over the net
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: How to Develop a Backhand Flick: Anna Bright's Complete Guide
items.summary: Anna Bright went from zero one-handed ability to developing one of the best backhand flicks in women's pickleball. Here's exactly how she did it and how you can too.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Q&A with rising young pickleball pro Cailyn Campbell
items.summary: Cailyn Campbell has been on the pro pickleball scene for more than a year, but she's still only 15 years old and is rapidly climbing the PPA Tour rankings. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Don't Sleep on the Boomstik Widebody
items.summary: The widebody Boomstik feels lighter and more stable in the hand while boasting a laughably large sweet spot. What's not to love?
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: How to Beat Bangers in Pickleball: The Complete 7-Strategy System
items.summary: This guide breaks down seven proven tactics to neutralize hard-driving opponents and win more matches.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Manipulate Pickleball Momentum to Control Every Point
items.summary: The difference between 5.0 players and everyone else isn't speed or strength—it's understanding pickleball momentum. Master the three states of every rally and you'll start beating players who are technically better than you.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Pickleball Training Plan for Competitive Players: Get Better in 90 Days
items.summary: Whether you're pushing from 3.5 to 4.0 or grinding toward 4.5, this week-by-week system gives you the structure to get there.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Handle the Wide Aggressive Dink in Pickleball
items.summary: When opponents pull you wide at the net, your instinct might be to speed it up or hit it cross-court, but that's exactly what gets you in trouble.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Pickleball Fitness for Seniors: Safe Training to Play Better and Last Longer
items.summary: Pickleball fitness for seniors is the difference between playing twice a week and playing every day without breaking down. This guide breaks down the exact training approach that lets older players compete harder and recover faster.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Are You Taking Your Pickleball Dinks Too Early or Too Late?
items.summary: For most pickleball dinks, the best contact point is near the top of the bounce, when the ball is high enough to guide cleanly but no longer springing upward. Letting it fall lower can help if you are late or stretched, but waiting too long often makes the dink harder to control.
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Treat and Prevent Sore Legs in Pickleball
items.summary: Sore legs from pickleball are usually caused by all the stopping, shuffling, lunging, and low positioning the sport demands, not just by how far you move. Mild soreness is common, but sharp pain, limping, swelling, or weakness are signs you should back off and take it more seriou
items.author: Ana

items.title: Should You Grunt in Pickleball? A Real Guide for Rec Players
items.summary: Grunting in pickleball is not automatically wrong, but loud, constant grunting can become distracting. A controlled exhale may help rhythm, looseness, and power, but rec players should keep it proportional. If the sound shows up on every dink, reset, and routine volley, it is pro
items.author: Ana

items.title: Proton paddles OK'd for use in PPA Tour, Major League Pickleball events, officials say
items.summary: Proton Pickleball and the United Pickleball Association have resolved their contract dispute, and Proton paddles are now permitted for use in PPA Tour and Major League Pickleball events going forward. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Anna Bright reveals which pro players have impressed her the most in 2026
items.summary: In a recent newsletter, Anna Bright went into detail on which pro players have impressed her the most in 2026. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: You Need Both of These Forehand Drops in Pickleball
items.summary: In pickleball, using two forehand drop variations helps you handle different opponents more effectively. A shorter, more topspin-heavy drop works better against players reaching in, while a deeper, more aggressive drop is useful against players who stay off the line and give you
items.author: Ana

items.title: Edgeless Pickleball Paddles: Pros, Cons, and Who Should Actually Buy One
items.summary: Edgeless pickleball paddles can feel quicker, cleaner, and less bulky around the perimeter, which some players love in fast hands battles. But they usually offer less built-in edge protection than traditional paddles, so they make the most sense for players who value speed and fe
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Play With a Banger in Pickleball
items.summary: To play well with a banger in pickleball, stop moving forward on autopilot. Read the quality of their drive, protect the middle, expect fast counters, and look to finish the next ball. The key is not changing their style — it is organizing your game around it more intelligently.
items.author: Ana

items.title: Ranking the best sibling duos in pickleball
items.summary: There are more than a dozen sibling duos in pro pickleball. We recently put our heads together to rank the Top 10. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: VAIR is rewriting how skill is measured in pickleball – and where players belong
items.summary: The VAIR pickleball rating system is designed to bring greater accuracy, transparency and structure to amateur and competitive play. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: PPA Tour Sacramento Open recap: Eric Oncins wins fist mixed gold, Hunter Johnson disqualified
items.summary: The PPA Tour Sacramento Open went down last week and we saw some crazy moments. Chris Cali from Sorry Not Sorry Pickleball is here to break it all down. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: The 5 Doubles Habits Better Players Punish Every Time
items.summary: A high-level player recently shared a brutally honest point: when he plays 4.0-level opponents, there are a few holes he sees over and over again — and he goes after them immediately. That matters for rec players because this is exactly how improvement often works in pickleball.
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Reset From the Baseline in Pickleball
items.summary: A baseline reset in pickleball is a soft defensive shot used when you are deep, under pressure, and not in a good position to attack. Instead of forcing a risky drive, it helps you slow the rally down, neutralize the attack, and stay in the point. A lot of rec players think of th
items.author: Ana

items.title: Pro pickleball case study: Why JW and Jorja Johnson have taken a step back in 2026
items.summary: JW Johnson and Jorja Johnson have had a down year by their standards in mixed doubles. In this article we take a closer look at a recent match to see what's causing them to fall short. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Pickleballers Season 3, Episode 10: Anna Bright on new partnerships and past breakups
items.summary: In this episode of Pickleballers, Anna Bright sits down with Roscoe and Jared to pull back the curtain on her journey from junior tennis to the top of pickleball. More

items.summary: The most recent PPA Tour Asia event -- the MB Hanoi Cup in Vietnam -- featured many of the top players from both the U.S. and overseas, highlighting the potential for future international team competitions. More

items.summary: A new reality series called "Partners" is set to be released next month, showcasing the inner-workings of pro pickleball and the players who compete on the PPA Tour. More

items.summary: Pro pickleball player James Ignatowich recently joined PBallers to discuss his PPA Tour termination at the end of 2025 and how he built his paddle brand -- RPM -- from scratch. More

items.title: JOOLA files patent infringement lawsuit against 11 other pickleball paddle manufacturers
items.summary: JOOLA Pickleball this week filed a patent infringement lawsuit against 11 paddle companies, alleging that the competitors copied JOOLA's "propulsion core technology." More
